title: Quantum Computing Methods Overcome Hardware Limits With Hybrid Classical Approaches
why:
- Researchers conducted a comprehensive survey and benchmarking of recent developments in the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) algorithm on NISQ devices using a 72-qubit superconducting processor.
- VQE employs hybrid quantum-classical approaches to mitigate the hardware limitations of current noisy intermediate-scale quantum computers.
- Adaptations of VQE—such as circuit complexity reduction, chemistry-inspired ansatzes, and extensions to excited states—help make the algorithm more scalable and broadly applicable, particularly in quantum chemistry simulations.
- Logical error rates in recent implementations reached 2.914% per cycle, marking a substantial improvement in quantum computation reliability.
- Quantum simulators on classical high-performance computing infrastructure were used to benchmark and validate quantum results, providing critical comparisons for algorithm development.
- Despite advancements, current devices with around 6,000 physical qubits are still far from the millions needed for fault-tolerant, transformative quantum applications, emphasizing the importance of hybrid and algorithmic innovations.
